evidence_quotes.quote: if I buy a video game I can still play it again in 10 years’ time.
in the Netherlands where all lootboxes are exiled.

paragraphs.text: Hallo, I would appreciate it if this law also ensures that if I buy a video game I can still play it again in 10 years’ time. I sometimes do this out of nostalgia and with how industry wants to make this impossible now, I would like to see it if they cannot legally remove something I have already paid for. I also fear that, in my view, the ‘addictive design of digital products’ will go too far as, in my view, in the Netherlands where all lootboxes are exiled. Personally, I believe that if you get what you get, you cannot in any way exchange with other people that there should be lighter rules, and that it should be allowed. I can understand what laws are in place to prevent the most addictive, but I do not want it to become completely illegal.
